understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to use a limited number of steps to maneuver the controllable piece(s) around a grid to reach the goal tile(s) (marked as '+'), potentially by rearranging blocks to open a path.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction, updating the piece's [x, y] pixel position when successful. Movement could fail when blocked by walls/boundaries or other blocking objects; the movement_blocked flags were only hints and not guarantees. Pushable objects (blocks) could be moved if they were immediately adjacent in the movement direction and there was space for them to be displaced along with the selected piece. Levels could include multiple selectable pieces; using CLICK with an object's exact pixel coordinate switched which piece was under control (as seen when control switched between pieces at [6, 21] and [21, 21]). Walls acted as fixed obstacles that could prevent movement or pushing depending on their behavior.
